name: bZxProtocol
report: The attacker exploited an inflation attack vector related to the `borrow` function in an empty or newly forked liquidity pool. By first depositing a minimal amount to acquire pool tokens and then front-running a legitimate large deposit transaction with a massive 'donation' (inflation) of assets, the attacker artificially and drastically inflated the value of each pool token. This manipulation allowed the attacker to subsequently use the `borrow` function (or a similar withdrawal mechanism) to illicitly withdraw a far greater quantity of tokens than they rightfully owned or had initially deposited.
